lines.gurmukhi: ਅਨਿਕ ਕਟਕ ਜੈਸੇ ਭੂਲਿ ਪਰੇ ਅਬ ਕਹਤੇ ਕਹਨੁ ਨ ਆਇਆ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: anik kattak jaise bhool pare ab kahate kehan na aaeaa |3|
lines.english: Like the many bracelets, which I mistakenly thought were gold; now, I do not say what I said then. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਰਾਜ ਭੁਇਅੰਗ ਪ੍ਰਸੰਗ ਜੈਸੇ ਹਹਿ ਅਬ ਕਛੁ ਮਰਮੁ ਜਨਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: raaj bhueiang prasang jaise heh ab kachh maram janaaeaa |
lines.english: Like the story of the rope mistaken for a snake, the mystery has now been explained to me.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਰਬੇ ਏਕੁ ਅਨੇਕੈ ਸੁਆਮੀ ਸਭ ਘਟ ਭੋੁਗਵੈ ਸੋਈ ॥
lines.translit: sarabe ek anekai suaamee sabh ghatt bhougavai soee |
lines.english: The One Lord is pervading the many forms; He enjoys Himself in all hearts.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਹਿ ਰਵਿਦਾਸ ਹਾਥ ਪੈ ਨੇਰੈ ਸਹਜੇ ਹੋਇ ਸੁ ਹੋਈ ॥੪॥੧॥
lines.translit: keh ravidaas haath pai nerai sahaje hoe su hoee |4|1|
lines.english: Says Ravi Daas, the Lord is nearer than our own hands and feet. Whatever will be, will be. ||4||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਉ ਹਮ ਬਾਂਧੇ ਮੋਹ ਫਾਸ ਹਮ ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਬਧਨਿ ਤੁਮ ਬਾਧੇ ॥
lines.translit: jau ham baandhe moh faas ham prem badhan tum baadhe |
lines.english: If I am bound by the noose of emotional attachment, then I shall bind You, Lord, with the bonds of love.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਪਨੇ ਛੂਟਨ ਕੋ ਜਤਨੁ ਕਰਹੁ ਹਮ ਛੂਟੇ ਤੁਮ ਆਰਾਧੇ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: apane chhoottan ko jatan karahu ham chhootte tum aaraadhe |1|
lines.english: Go ahead and try to escape, Lord; I have escaped by worshipping and adoring You. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਬ ਕਹਾ ਕਰਹੁਗੇ ਐਸੀ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: ab kahaa karahuge aisee |1| rahaau |
lines.english: Now, what will You do? ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਾਧਵੇ ਜਾਨਤ ਹਹੁ ਜੈਸੀ ਤੈਸੀ ॥
lines.translit: maadhave jaanat hahu jaisee taisee |
lines.english: O Lord, You know my love for You.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮੀਨੁ ਪਕਰਿ ਫਾਂਕਿਓ ਅਰੁ ਕਾਟਿਓ ਰਾਂਧਿ ਕੀਓ ਬਹੁ ਬਾਨੀ ॥
lines.translit: meen pakar faankio ar kaattio raandh keeo bahu baanee |
lines.english: A fish is caught, cut up, and cooked it in many different ways.

lines.gurmukhi: ਖੰਡ ਖੰਡ ਕਰਿ ਭੋਜਨੁ ਕੀਨੋ ਤਊ ਨ ਬਿਸਰਿਓ ਪਾਨੀ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: khandd khandd kar bhojan keeno taoo na bisario paanee |2|
lines.english: Bit by bit, it is eaten, but still, it does not forget the water. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਪਨ ਬਾਪੈ ਨਾਹੀ ਕਿਸੀ ਕੋ ਭਾਵਨ ਕੋ ਹਰਿ ਰਾਜਾ ॥
lines.translit: aapan baapai naahee kisee ko bhaavan ko har raajaa |
lines.english: The Lord, our King, is father to no one, except those who love Him.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮੋਹ ਪਟਲ ਸਭੁ ਜਗਤੁ ਬਿਆਪਿਓ ਭਗਤ ਨਹੀ ਸੰਤਾਪਾ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: moh pattal sabh jagat biaapio bhagat nahee santaapaa |3|
lines.english: The veil of emotional attachment has been cast over the entire world, but it does not bother the Lord's devotee. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਹਿ ਰਵਿਦਾਸ ਭਗਤਿ ਇਕ ਬਾਢੀ ਅਬ ਇਹ ਕਾ ਸਿਉ ਕਹੀਐ ॥
lines.translit: keh ravidaas bhagat ik baadtee ab ih kaa siau kaheeai |
lines.english: Says Ravi Daas, my devotion to the One Lord is increasing; now, who can I tell this to?

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਾ ਕਾਰਨਿ ਹਮ ਤੁਮ ਆਰਾਧੇ ਸੋ ਦੁਖੁ ਅਜਹੂ ਸਹੀਐ ॥੪॥੨॥
lines.translit: jaa kaaran ham tum aaraadhe so dukh ajahoo saheeai |4|2|
lines.english: That which brought me to worship and adore You - I am still suffering that pain. ||4||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਦੁਲਭ ਜਨਮੁ ਪੁੰਨ ਫਲ ਪਾਇਓ ਬਿਰਥਾ ਜਾਤ ਅਬਿਬੇਕੈ ॥
lines.translit: dulabh janam pun fal paaeo birathaa jaat abibekai |
lines.english: I obtained this precious human life as a reward for my past actions, but without discriminating wisdom, it is wasted in vain.

lines.gurmukhi: ਰਾਜੇ ਇੰਦ੍ਰ ਸਮਸਰਿ ਗ੍ਰਿਹ ਆਸਨ ਬਿਨੁ ਹਰਿ ਭਗਤਿ ਕਹਹੁ ਕਿਹ ਲੇਖੈ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: raaje indr samasar grih aasan bin har bhagat kahahu kih lekhai |1|
lines.english: Tell me, without devotional worship of the Lord, of what use are mansions and thrones like those of King Indra? ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਨ ਬੀਚਾਰਿਓ ਰਾਜਾ ਰਾਮ ਕੋ ਰਸੁ ॥
lines.translit: n beechaario raajaa raam ko ras |
lines.english: You have not considered the sublime essence of the Name of the Lord, our King;

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਾਨਿ ਅਜਾਨ ਭਏ ਹਮ ਬਾਵਰ ਸੋਚ ਅਸੋਚ ਦਿਵਸ ਜਾਹੀ ॥
lines.translit: jaan ajaan bhe ham baavar soch asoch divas jaahee |
lines.english: We do not know what we need to know, and we have become insane. We do not consider what we should consider; our days are passing away.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਹ ਰਸ ਅਨ ਰਸ ਬੀਸਰਿ ਜਾਹੀ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: jih ras an ras beesar jaahee |1| rahaau |
lines.english: this sublime essence shall cause you to forget all other essences. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਇੰਦ੍ਰੀ ਸਬਲ ਨਿਬਲ ਬਿਬੇਕ ਬੁਧਿ ਪਰਮਾਰਥ ਪਰਵੇਸ ਨਹੀ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: eindree sabal nibal bibek budh paramaarath paraves nahee |2|
lines.english: Our passions are strong, and our discriminating intellect is weak; we have no access to the supreme objective. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਹਿ ਰਵਿਦਾਸ ਉਦਾਸ ਦਾਸ ਮਤਿ ਪਰਹਰਿ ਕੋਪੁ ਕਰਹੁ ਜੀਅ ਦਇਆ ॥੩॥੩॥
lines.translit: keh ravidaas udaas daas mat parahar kop karahu jeea deaa |3|3|
lines.english: Says Ravi Daas, Your slave, O Lord, I am disillusioned and detached; please, spare me Your anger, and have mercy on my soul. ||3||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਹੀਅਤ ਆਨ ਅਚਰੀਅਤ ਅਨ ਕਛੁ ਸਮਝ ਨ ਪਰੈ ਅਪਰ ਮਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: kaheeat aan achareeat an kachh samajh na parai apar maaeaa |
lines.english: We say one thing, and do something else; entangled in endless Maya, we do not understand anything.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੁਖ ਸਾਗਰੁ ਸੁਰਤਰ ਚਿੰਤਾਮਨਿ ਕਾਮਧੇਨੁ ਬਸਿ ਜਾ ਕੇ ॥
lines.translit: sukh saagar suratar chintaaman kaamadhen bas jaa ke |
lines.english: He is the ocean of peace; the miraculous tree of life, the wish-fulfilling jewel, and the Kaamadhayna, the cow which fulfills all desires, all are in His power.

lines.gurmukhi: ਚਾਰਿ ਪਦਾਰਥ ਅਸਟ ਦਸਾ ਸਿਧਿ ਨਵ ਨਿਧਿ ਕਰ ਤਲ ਤਾ ਕੇ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: chaar padaarath asatt dasaa sidh nav nidh kar tal taa ke |1|
lines.english: The four great blessings, the eighteen supernatural spiritual powers of the Siddhas, and the nine treasures, are all in the palm of His hand. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਵਰ ਸਭ ਤਿਆਗਿ ਬਚਨ ਰਚਨਾ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: avar sabh tiaag bachan rachanaa |1| rahaau |
lines.english: Abandon your involvement in all other words. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਹਰਿ ਹਰਿ ਨ ਜਪਹਿ ਰਸਨਾ ॥
lines.translit: har har har na japeh rasanaa |
lines.english: You do not chant with your tongue the Name of the Lord, Har, Har, Har.

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਿਆਸ ਬਿਚਾਰਿ ਕਹਿਓ ਪਰਮਾਰਥੁ ਰਾਮ ਨਾਮ ਸਰਿ ਨਾਹੀ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: biaas bichaar kahio paramaarath raam naam sar naahee |2|
lines.english: After deep contemplation, Vyaas spoke of the supreme objective; there is nothing equal to the Lord's Name. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਾ ਖਿਆਨ ਪੁਰਾਨ ਬੇਦ ਬਿਧਿ ਚਉਤੀਸ ਅਖਰ ਮਾਂਹੀ ॥
lines.translit: naanaa khiaan puraan bed bidh chautees akhar maanhee |
lines.english: The various Shaastras, Puranaas, and the Vedas of Brahma, are made up of thirty-four letters.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਹਜ ਸਮਾਧਿ ਉਪਾਧਿ ਰਹਤ ਫੁਨਿ ਬਡੈ ਭਾਗਿ ਲਿਵ ਲਾਗੀ ॥
lines.translit: sehaj samaadh upaadh rehat fun baddai bhaag liv laagee |
lines.english: Very fortunate are those who are absorbed in celestial bliss, and released from their entanglements; they are lovingly attached to the Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਉ ਤੁਮ ਗਿਰਿਵਰ ਤਉ ਹਮ ਮੋਰਾ ॥
lines.translit: jau tum girivar tau ham moraa |
lines.english: If You are the mountain, Lord, then I am the peacock.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਹਿ ਰਵਿਦਾਸ ਪ੍ਰਗਾਸੁ ਰਿਦੈ ਧਰਿ ਜਨਮ ਮਰਨ ਭੈ ਭਾਗੀ ॥੩॥੪॥
lines.translit: keh ravidaas pragaas ridai dhar janam maran bhai bhaagee |3|4|
lines.english: Says Ravi Daas, enshrine the Lord's Light within your heart, and your fear of birth and death shall run away from you. ||3||4||

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਾਧਵੇ ਤੁਮ ਨ ਤੋਰਹੁ ਤਉ ਹਮ ਨਹੀ ਤੋਰਹਿ ॥
lines.translit: maadhave tum na torahu tau ham nahee toreh |
lines.english: O Lord, if You will not break with me, then I will not break with You.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਉ ਤੁਮ ਚੰਦ ਤਉ ਹਮ ਭਏ ਹੈ ਚਕੋਰਾ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: jau tum chand tau ham bhe hai chakoraa |1|
lines.english: If You are the moon, then I am the partridge in love with it. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਉ ਤੁਮ ਤੀਰਥ ਤਉ ਹਮ ਜਾਤੀ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: jau tum teerath tau ham jaatee |2|
lines.english: If You are the sacred place of pilgrimage, then I am the pilgrim. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਤੁਮ ਸਿਉ ਤੋਰਿ ਕਵਨ ਸਿਉ ਜੋਰਹਿ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: tum siau tor kavan siau joreh |1| rahaau |
lines.english: For, if I were to break with You, with whom would I then join? ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਉ ਤੁਮ ਦੀਵਰਾ ਤਉ ਹਮ ਬਾਤੀ ॥
lines.translit: jau tum deevaraa tau ham baatee |
lines.english: If You are the lamp, then I am the wick.
